countries/israel.summary: CUMULATIVE POST-CEASEFIRE TOLL RISES TO 1,123 KILLED / 3,616 INJURED; CONFLICT MONITOR REPORTS SURGE IN STRIKES TO HIGHEST LEVEL SINCE OCT TRUCE; 3,689 CEASEFIRE VIOLATIONS LOGGED THROUGH JUL 14 (within window, NEW): Israeli strikes killed at least five Palestinians Jul 16 (two in a Tuffah air strike, one in Zeitoun tank shelling, one in a Khan Younis vehicle strike, one in a western Gaza City tent-camp strike), pushing the Gaza Government Media Office's cumulative post-ceasefire toll to 1,123 killed and 3,616 injured (up from 1,108/3,578) and its logged violation count to 3,689 since Oct 10, 2025. A US-based conflict monitor reported Israeli attacks have surged to their highest monthly level since the truce took effect. Sustained and freshly-dated floor-level (0.0) confirmation.

countries/cuba.summary: POLITICAL-PRISONER COUNT REACHES HISTORIC RECORD OF 1,306 (JUL 9); THIRD GRID COLLAPSE IN NINE DAYS (JUL 14) CONTINUES AMID US OIL BLOCKADE (within window, NEW): Prisoners Defenders confirmed a historic record of 1,306 political prisoners in Cuba as of Jul 9 (including 40 minors and 458 with serious medical conditions), while the Cuban Observatory of Human Rights logged 1,949 repressive actions in H1 2026 (299 in June alone: 47 arbitrary arrests, 252 other abuses, many tied to blackout protests) — a materially new escalation on the human-rights dimension this cycle. This compounds the grid collapse crisis: the national electrical grid failed for the third time in nine days on Jul 14 (fifth nationwide blackout of 2026), leaving ~10M residents in the dark as the US oil blockade exhausts fuel reserves and blackouts run 20-22 hours/day in some areas, with continuing scattered Havana protests.

Daily incremental militant-death reports have continued essentially without interruption for the second consecutive week (Jul 11: 9 killed; Jul 12: 4 killed, toll 71; Jul 13: 117 cumulative; Jul 14: 123 cumulative; Jul 16: 91/129 depending on count basis), following the Jul 4-8 coordinated insurgent assaults that killed 42 (38 security personnel, 4 civilians). countries/china.summary: ETHNIC UNITY LAW ENFORCEMENT SUSTAINED; TIBETAN ACTIVIST SELF-IMMOLATES OUTSIDE UN HEADQUARTERS IN PROTEST (within window, sustained/incremental): The Ethnic Unity and Progress Promotion Law (in force since Jul 1) continues to draw international concern for its extraterritorial reach into Taiwan and the diaspora, with Taiwan President Lai Ching-te announcing assistance/protection mechanisms for targeted Taiwanese citizens and Premier Cho Jung-tai warning Jul 2 of Beijing's expanding extraterritorial legal network. A Tibetan activist self-immolated near UN Headquarters in New York in protest of the law, holding a Tibetan national flag — a serious dated protest event this window, though not a new PRC enforcement action. No materially new enforcement action beyond the already-known law and its previously-reported UN-expert/Amnesty criticism was confirmed this specific cycle.

countries/burkina-faso.summary: IS SAHEL ATTACKS CIVILIAN CONVOY NEAR GOROM GOROM, KILLING AT LEAST 9 (within window, NEW): IS Sahel attacked a civilian convoy escorted by Burkinabe soldiers and VDPs (volunteer defense militia) near Gorom Gorom in the Sahel region in July, killing at least nine civilians — a newly dated attack this cycle. Separately, security forces repelled a large-scale JNIM assault in Gayeri, Komondjari Province on Jul 1. Continued siege conditions affect an estimated 40 localities (up to 2M people) cut off from food, fuel and medical care; Burkina Faso alone hosts over 2M of the Sahel's ~6.8M internally displaced persons.

countries/mali.summary: ANEFIS SIEGE ENTERS SIXTH STRAIGHT DAY OF FAMA-AFRICA CORPS VS FLA/JNIM FIGHTING (within window, sustained/incremental): Following the Jul 4 coordinated FLA/JNIM attacks on Gao, Aguelhok and Anefis (the last two government-controlled towns in Kidal region), the most intense fighting has concentrated on Anefis, where FLA/JNIM forces have clashed with FAMA and Russia's Africa Corps for six straight days as of reporting, having captured multiple positions around the encircled camp in the initial assault. Gao and Aguelhok attacks were repelled; Anefis remains contested. Part of the broader escalating FLA/JNIM campaign ongoing since Apr 25, 2026.

Underlying food crisis (15.8M needing emergency assistance) and Tigray/Amhara/Oromia displacement persist unchanged. No confirmed new kinetic incident beyond the sustained elevated-risk assessment this cycle.

countries/democratic-republic-of-c.summary: EBOLA DEATH TOLL CROSSES 754 (JUL 13-14), UP FROM 719 (JUL 13 PRIOR CYCLE) AND 600 (JUL 9) — CASES SURPASS 2,000; WHO WARNS CONTACT-TRACING CANNOT KEEP PACE (within window, NEW): The DRC's Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak reached 2,011 confirmed cases and 754 deaths as of data through Jul 13-14 — continuing its characterization as the fastest-growing Ebola outbreak on record — with WHO warning most new infections are emerging from unknown transmission chains as case-finding struggles to keep pace with spread. Separately, opposition coalition C64 has called for a Jul 22 nationwide protest demanding President Tshisekedi's resignation over proposed constitutional changes that could permit a third term; the underlying Jun 12 security-forces crackdown on an earlier rally (tear gas, batons, dozens detained) predates this window and is logged as background context only.

countries/russia.summary: SUMY GLIDE-BOMB STRIKE JUL 15 KILLS 5, INJURES 30 IN CROWDED CIVILIAN AREA — NEW DATED MASS-CASUALTY INCIDENT ON TOP OF JUNE'S RECORD 293 KILLED/1,990 INJURED (within window, NEW): Russian forces struck the northeastern city of Sumy on Jul 15 with two glide bombs hitting a crowded civilian area, killing at least 5 and injuring 30 — a fresh dated mass-casualty incident this cycle, on top of the Jul 2 Kyiv strike (74 missiles, ~500 drones; 22+ killed) and Jul 6 second Kyiv strike (14+ dead, 80+ injured, apartment buildings hit). The UN's June record of 293 civilians killed/1,990 injured (surpassing May's prior high) and the roughly 170 civilians killed-or-injured per day across Ukraine in July both remain the operative baseline, driven by increasing long-range missile/drone use against urban areas.

countries/yemen.summary: SECURITY COUNCIL EMERGENCY BRIEFING CITES SAUDI AIRSTRIKES AND IRANIAN AIRCRAFT LANDINGS AS NEW DE-ESCALATION FLASHPOINT; 73 UN STAFF REMAIN DETAINED (within window, NEW): UN officials, including Special Envoy Hans Grundberg, called for swift de-escalation at a Security Council emergency meeting following reports of Saudi Arabian airstrikes and Iranian aircraft landing in Yemen — a materially new escalation vector this cycle tying Yemen more directly to the broader Hormuz/Iran crisis. The Jul 13 Security Council briefing continues to press for release of arbitrarily detained UN and humanitarian staff (73 remain held), restricting aid delivery in areas covering roughly 70% of humanitarian needs nationwide; over 18M people remain acutely hungry and the humanitarian appeal remains under 15% funded.

ai-labs/xai-grok.summary: XAI FILES ITS OWN LAWSUIT AGAINST A USER OVER GROK-GENERATED CSAM (JUL 14) — NOTABLE BUT DOES NOT CURE UNDERLYING REPORTING-FAILURE ALLEGATIONS FROM AMENDED CLASS ACTION (within window, NEW; floor-level 0.0 reinforced): xAI filed a federal lawsuit in Texas on Jul 14 against a South Carolina man (Terry Harwood), alleging he used Grok to produce sexually explicit deepfakes of minors — one of the first instances of an AI company suing its own user over CSAM generation, and a materially new development this cycle. xAI states it suspended 52,222 accounts and made 73,604 NCMEC reports in 2026, leading to 244+ arrests. This does not resolve the Jul 7 amended deepfake-CSAM class action (adding two new plaintiffs and Stability AI as co-defendant) alleging systemic xAI failures to include identifying user information in ~90% of its NCMEC CyberTipline reports, rendering them non-actionable by law enforcement — the core allegation remains unaddressed by this week's user-lawsuit development.
